author: @jacobi7773
text: Honestly I really wanted to like this show. I really did. I'm a huge fan of the Grumps and everything they do, but damn was this a dud. A ton of the jokes barely land, most, if not all of the characters feel flat, and the story is really bare bones. It's the most underdog story you could imagine. Some of the comedy works. But it's mainly little one liners that Dan or Arin say. The rest of the cast seem like strange character traits just made into a character without giving them anymore depth than that. Then there's the game that they play. I swear, if you cut together a video of all the screen time that this game gets in the entire show you wouldn't even have a full minute of footage. It's ridiculous. They keep referring to the game the entire show since it's literally the center piece. The most you ever really find out is that this game is a Moba and that it's Hard. I've watched the show twice and that's the extent that I know. They even have Dan, the guy who literally knows nothing about the central plot, until they have to explain it, and they barely even explain it to him. Like, it's the perfect opportunity to tell anyone who doesn't play Mobas how they work. You can get a pretty good idea if you play games like League of Legends, but if not then you're screwed. Just from watching the trailer you can basically figure out the entire plot of the show. "A rag tag group of gamers get together and form an Esports team. Despite all the odds against them, they manage to stick it out in the end and pull off the win at the big tournament." The only thing I left out is a twist to the ending that still brought no depth to the show. It's just a slight change to how you think it would happen. But nothing changes, it's not anymore satisfying to watch. It feels like an awkward high school play with some okay acting and humor that only sometimes lands. If they continue this show (because with as open as an ending as they had they totally could) I hope they improve the writing of the show more than anything.

People bash YouTubers for doing this but i don't get it. You don't have to pretend that its not Danny and Arin to make a point about whether or not you will watch it. They are in it. There is nothing wrong with doing a Youtube Red show. and hey they make some money off of it. Its not like they are quitting Game Grumps. Not only does it look funny but it appeals to those who watch their channel, Gamers. If you call them sellouts that just rude and you honestly shouldn't. And lets be real here, if they make money off of this then Game Grumps can improve. author: @someretard5356
text: Listen, stop hating, please. What is this doing for you? They've been busting their asses for the past 5 years to bring you funny content, and the second things change even a little bit you piss your pants. You need to realize that the same thing over and over again gets tiring, and this show provides the grumps with a new intuitive way to have fun and be a part of something bigger. Plus, it's not like they're discarding their old ways, they still play games and have fun as always. Sure, it's YouTube Red and practically everything has been a letdown so far and you don't want to pay monthly for it. Neither do I, but that shouldn't drive you to call out the Grumps for going on it. If you're not even bothering to see the full product, what gives you the right to talk shit about it? Honestly, just because most of the shows on Red are shit, these are the GRUMPS. Give them a chance and let them be free to do whatever they wish to do. It's not your place to judge the show they're putting so much work into being produced. They've already set their minds to it, and your commentary won't do a single thing. I'll say it again, please, save your hate and just give it a chance, and if you don't have Red, don't hate at all, because you're not going to even see the final product. Thank you and good day.

author: @onarionaa8359
text:
to me, there's a reason our favorite YouTubers' shows and movies often fail: *they aren't the same comedy style we love from them. the movies/shows are stylized instead of improv, and aren't as vulgar.*

take Rhett and Link, or Shane Dawson as examples. i'd much rather watch these entertainers just hanging out than watch a TV show/movie they're starring in — because these people are not EXPERIENCED making movies and TV shows. 

and so they have to give up the creative direction to someone who is experienced...someone who doesn't understand the sense of humor and fucks it up.

since i'm a big fan of Rick and Morty and Community, Dan Harmon's name made me 😃 but he's only producing. 

i think Dan Harmon, more than anyone, should know that what he's making here will be no better than Season 4 of Community...

if YouTube Red shows don't want to capture the real heart of the comedy of the YouTubers they use — Red is going to fail.
